title: calochlaena dubia
intro: Calochlaena dubia, also known as the black lipped fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to eastern Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calochlaena dubia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.
